mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment rate crossing the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60d returns, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 52% over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles produce positive 5d returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal loses statistical significance in regime-filtered backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL increase above 0.4% signals accelerating inflation that forces equity valuation compression, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above the threshold consistently precede positive 20d returns, or the predictive IC remains negative across all tested lookback periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity and rising corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60d forward returns over a 4-year rolling window, or Rank IC stays below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales, where RSAFS declines while GDPC1 remains positive, indicates weakening consumer demand ahead of official revisions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict negative returns, with SPX posting positive 20d returns in > 58% of triggered instances over rolling 3-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase indicates capacity utilization improvements and margin expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases yield negative 5d returns in > 55% of cases, or the signal Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in regime-specific out-of-sample tests.
